Technology Assessment Framework

The framework will describe a standardized evidence-based methodology for evaluating wastewater treatment technologies using engineering, statistical, compliance, reliability, and conditional reliability analyses.
More content will be added as the framework evolves.

My current interests include sharing my statistical inquiries and technical reports I’ve worked that may help others solve similar problems related to applied statistics.

title: About
text:
🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.
Hello! I’m Vince Pileggi, a wastewater engineering expert, with the Ontario Ministry fo the Environment, Conservation and Parks and adjunct professor at the University of Guelph, School of Engineering.
Aside from my regular work I’m particularly passionate about the correct application of statistics to solving engineering problems when appropriate. I use RStudio [@rstudio] for R development, Quarto [@quarto] for technical publishing and of course \(\LaTeX\) [@latex] for high-quality typesetting.
My dissertation was focused on municipal sludge treatment using an anaerobic membrane bioreactor and is available though my Research Gate profile [@pileggi_researchgate] and at the Uni. The thesis was written in \(LaTeX\) but included as significant amount of R-coding and statistics. It was a lot of fun with some frustration finishing it off 😄!
This project hosts statistical analyses and technical notes. Built with Quarto and published via GitHub Pages (Option A, /docs on main).

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text: 🚧 This website is actively being built. Some pages may be incomplete or move.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Evidence-Based Engineering
I develop and share educational resources, research notes, and reproducible methods at the intersection of environmental engineering, applied statistics, and technical communication.
This site focuses on public, non-confidential material related to engineering statistics, wastewater research, teaching, publications, and reproducible workflows. It does not contain internal reviews, regulatory assessments, or unpublished work prepared through my government employment.






Note



Material on this site reflects my academic and professional interests, including my adjunct professor role at the University of Guelph. It does not represent the views, policies, or positions of my employer.

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Current Focus


Engineering Statistics
Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including reliability analysis, uncertainty, visualization, and reproducible reporting.
Explore methods


Wastewater Research
Public research and educational material related to wastewater treatment, environmental monitoring, and data-driven engineering assessment.
View research


Teaching and Mentoring
Resources for students, engineers, and early-career professionals interested in applying statistics and reproducible workflows to engineering problems.
Teaching resources


Reproducible Workflows
Examples using Quarto, R, Python, GitHub, and open technical writing tools for transparent engineering analysis and communication.
Learn more

title: Evidence-Based Engineering
text:
Guiding Principle

Engineering decisions are strongest when they are supported by transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty.
